slices.name: LOD Bucketing
slices.intent: Add screen-space DAG LOD bucketing and prove it with the narrowest static and smoke coverage before any new screenshot work.
testsToWrite: Add or tighten pure tests for resolveWorkplaneLod(projectedPlaneSpanPx).
Add or tighten pure tests for bucketVisibleDagNodes(...).
Extend scripts/test/dag-view-smoke.ts to prove close and zoomed-out DAG states using exported DAG datasets before any screenshot work.

taskQueue.title: Export active DAG LOD buckets
taskQueue.intent: Export the active DAG LOD bucket ids or counts through the browser dataset contract so smoke tests can assert live state directly.
doneWhen: The app exports live DAG LOD bucket ids or counts for the canonical DAG view through browser-readable dataset fields.
The smoke flow no longer needs to reconstruct those bucket ids from imported production helpers.
notes: Keep the change inside the browser dataset contract first.
Do not broaden into screenshot work or control-pad coverage.

taskQueue.title: Assert root and selected exported buckets
taskQueue.intent: Tighten dag-view-smoke so it asserts exported root-overview and selected-workplane LOD buckets directly from browser data.
doneWhen: The smoke flow reads exported bucket fields instead of recomputing buckets in test code.
The browser proof covers a root graph-point state and a selected title-only state.
notes: Use the smallest browser assertions that prove the exported fields are correct.
Keep static tests unchanged unless a tiny guard is clearly necessary.

taskQueue.title: Prove label-point and full-workplane states
taskQueue.intent: Extend static and focused smoke coverage to prove the remaining label-points and full-workplane states for the canonical DAG.
doneWhen: Static and smoke coverage prove label-points and full-workplane states in addition to graph-point and title-only.
README.md records the broadened Slice 6 proof without claiming screenshot readiness.
notes: Only broaden after the exported bucket contract is already in place.
Keep the canonical DAG flow as the source of truth.

datasets: README.md is the workflow source of truth.
The current task packet and rubric carry the slice and test ladder.
The canonical five-workplane network DAG fixture is already available and should be reused.
monitorFocus: Did the worker read README.md first?
Did the worker add or tighten the smallest relevant test before broadening implementation?
Did the worker keep the diff inside the owned files?
Do the check results actually prove the claimed new invariant?
Does the live GitHub Pages smoke result stay visible in the run artifacts?
